summary: Epic Dark Star marathon with tight jams and stellar playing
blurb: The show erupts in a monster Dark Star that rolls into China Cat Sunflower and I Know You Rider, while the first set delivers Bird Song and solid rockers like Cumberland Blues. Jerry's guitar soars, Phil's bass booms, and Keith’s piano adds texture throughout.
review: September 24, 1972 at The Palace Theater captures the Dead in peak improvisational form. The first set builds momentum with a crisp rendition of Bird Song, a swinging Cumberland Blues and a punchy Sugaree, but it’s the second‑set marathon that defines this night – a roughly 45‑minute Dark Star that seamlessly morphs into China Cat Sunflower and then into I Know You Rider before the band launches into a bright Sugar Magnolia. Reviewers repeatedly note Jerry Garcia’s perfect tone and expressive wah‑solo work driving the jams, while Phil Lesh’s thunderous bass underpins the extended explorations and even steps forward on vocals. Keith Godchaux supplies tasteful piano fills that color the improvisations without crowding them, and Bill Kreutzmann’s tight, short‑sweet drumming (with occasional Mickey Hart accents) keeps the energy propulsive. Highlights also include a fiery Playing In The Band, a gritty Jack Straw, and a heartfelt Friend Of The Devil, making this Palace show a must‑listen snapshot of the band’s 1972 chemistry. The JeffM soundboard source offers the cleanest mix with only minor blips, delivering the most balanced listening experience.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four recordings are overwhelmingly positive; three are clearly positive, praising the epic Dark Star > China Cat Sunflower > Rider sequence, tight band interplay and standout individual performances. The lone mixed audience tape offers no criticism of the musical performance, only recording clarity issues. Consistent high praise across multiple sources justifies a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with a well‑balanced mix, minimal cuts and the clearest capture of the long Dark Star jam.

key_highlights: Monster ~45‑minute Dark Star that flows into China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
Tight first‑set work on Bird Song, Cumberland Blues and Sugaree
Keith’s tasteful piano/harpsichord vibe adds depth to the improvisations
High‑energy Playing In The Band jam and solid rockers like Jack Straw

band_performance.Jerry: Perfect tone, expressive wah‑solo work and relentless drive that steered the epic Dark Star and kept the jams razor‑sharp.
band_performance.Phil: Booming, thunderous bass that dominated the Dark Star stretch; also stepped forward on vocals, adding a powerful presence.
band_performance.Bob: Solid rhythm guitar chords and steady vocal support; launched El Paso and acted as a reliable engine throughout the set.
band_performance.Keith: Tasteful piano fills with an acoustic‑piano/harpsichord vibe; supportive electric piano lines that enriched the improvisations without overwhelming them.
band_performance.Mickey: Occasional accents that complemented Bill’s tight drumming, adding subtle texture to the long jams.
band_performance.Billy: Tight, short‑sweet drumming that drove the extended Dark Star and kept the band locked in during high‑octane sections.
